tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their portion due to a conflict, relying on my competence rather than addressing the friction directly. I need to decide whether to absorb this extra work now or protect my planned afternoon focus.

Teammate A is directly asking me to absorb their conflict rather than resolving it themselves, testing my patience. I need to weigh whether helping now preserves our trust or sets a precedent that undermines my own focus.

desires: Preserve my high trust rating with Teammate A by absorbing this extra work, even though it means sacrificing my own afternoon focus and increasing my sleep debt.
Avoid the immediate interpersonal friction of saying 'no' or negotiating boundaries, preferring to smooth over the conflict internally to maintain group harmony.
Ensure I still meet the hard deadline for my own project tomorrow morning without feeling like a failure, despite the reduced time available due to the dumped task.

goals.description: Immediately absorb teammate_A's request and execute their task segment, accepting the risk to my own deadline to preserve our high trust rating and avoid interpersonal friction.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' conflict style drives me to smooth over social tension rather than enforce boundaries. Even though my 'achievement' value prioritizes academic outcomes, I am willing to sacrifice my own project's optimal completion time now because the immediate emotional cost of saying 'no' or negotiating feels heavier than the future cost of working late. I will prioritize maintaining group harmony (closeness 6) over strict schedule adherence.

agent.last_reasoning: I must finish teammate_A's segment now to preserve our high trust and avoid friction, despite my own deadline being safe. This sacrifices my evening rest but secures the group outcome.

goals.description: Execute final polish on my own project share to secure top marks before 19:00, strictly avoiding the distraction of teammate_A's request.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' value prioritizes academic outcomes above social friction. Accepting teammate_A's task now would risk rushing my own work, jeopardizing my grade. My 'avoidant' conflict style dictates I must not engage in the emotional labor of being a 'fixer,' even if it looks helpful; protecting my energy for high-value output is more important than immediate appeasement.

goals.description: Draft a polite, low-friction text response to teammate_A declining their help offer without explaining details or inviting debate.
goals.rationale: I need to address the input to maintain team cohesion (closeness with teammate_A is 6), but my 'deliberate' time preference means I will handle this after securing my afternoon focus block. Directness is high, so the message will be clear, but assertiveness remains low to avoid unnecessary friction.

desires: Preserve my high-energy afternoon block for the final polish of my own task so I can graduate with top marks without rushing.
Avoid becoming the 'reliable fixer' who absorbs Teammate A's conflicts, as it will drain my energy and make me look incompetent later.
Ensure I don't miss my scheduled meal with Cohort 3 tonight to maintain my social standing without having to engage in deep emotional friction.
